basic_details.job_title: TGPSC Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 10 Posts
basic_details.department: Town and Country Planning Department, Telangana
basic_details.short_information: The Telangana Public Service Commission (TGPSC) has released Notification No. 05/G/TP/2026, dated 06 July 2026, inviting online applications for 10 vacancies of Assistant Director of Town Planning in the Town and Country Planning Department, Government of Telangana. Eligible candidates can apply online at www.tgpsc.gov.in from 10 July 2026 and 17 August 2026. This article explains every important detail of the notification, including vacancy break-up, eligibility conditions, exam pattern, fee structure, and the complete Apply Online procedure.

important_dates.admit_card_date: 7 days prior to the examination, up to 5:00 PM on the previous day of the examination

application_fee.examination_fee: Rs 120 (all unemployed applicants are exempted; all Government/PSU/Corporation employees must pay this fee)
application_fee.details: Fee is payable online only, through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. The fee once remitted shall not be refunded or adjusted under any circumstances, and candidature will not be considered unless both the application processing fee and examination fee (wherever applicable) are paid in full.

age_limit.details: Age is reckoned as on 1 July 2026. The prescribed age limit is 18 to 44 years (the upper age limit having been raised from 34 to 44 years vide G.O.Ms.No.86, G.A.(Ser.A) Department, dated 18/05/2026). Candidates for the minimum age should not be born after 01/07/2008, and for the maximum age should not be born before 02/07/1982. | No person is eligible if he/she is less than 18 years of age or has crossed 61 years (superannuation age), even after availing eligible age relaxations.

qualification.details: Candidates must possess the prescribed academic qualification as on the date of notification, i.e. 06 July 2026. | Minimum qualification of B.Arch., or B.E./B.Tech. (Civil), or B.Planning/B.Tech. (Planning), or M.A. (Geography), or M.Sc. (Geography), or M.Sc. (Geoinformatics); AND A Post Graduate Degree or Diploma in Town and Country Planning from a University in India established or incorporated by or under a Central Act, State Act, or Provincial Act, or Fellowship/Associateship of the Institute of Town Planners of India, or equivalent membership of a professional Institute of Planners recognised by the University Grants Commission. | Degrees obtained through Open Universities/Distance Education mode must be recognised by the UGC/AICTE/Distance Education Bureau, as applicable. Candidates claiming an equivalent qualification will have their claim examined by an Expert Committee constituted by the Commission, whose decision is final.

pattern_table.Paper: Paper-I: General Studies and General Abilities
Paper-II: Subject Concerned (Common to All) (P.G. Level)

exam_pattern.details: Selection is based on a Written Examination (Objective Type), conducted in OMR-based offline mode or Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT) mode, as decided by the Commission. | Paper-I covers Current Affairs, General Science, Environmental Issues and Disaster Management, Economic and Social Development, Geography, History, Indian Constitution and Polity, Social Exclusion and Rights Issues, Telangana Society and Culture, Logical Reasoning and Data Interpretation, and Basic English. Paper-II (Subject Concerned, P.G. Level) covers core Town and Country Planning subjects such as Settlement Evolution and Planning Theory, Planning Analysis and Techniques, Infrastructure Planning, Traffic and Transportation Planning, Housing and Community Planning, Metropolitan and Regional Planning, Urban Information Systems and GIS, Environmental Planning, Urban Governance, Planning Legislation, Urban Design and Conservation, Project Planning and Appraisal, and Disaster Risk Mitigation. | Note: Merely securing the minimum qualifying marks does not vest any right in a candidate for being considered for final selection. Scores in multi-session CBRT examinations are normalised as per the formula given in Annexure-VII of the notification, and results are announced on a percentile basis.

how_to_apply: One Time Registration (OTR) – First-time applicants must register their bio-data on the TGPSC website to obtain a TGPSC ID; already registered applicants can log in directly using their existing TGPSC ID. Note: OTR is not considered an application for any post.

Login & Verify Details – Visit the Online Application link, log in using the TGPSC ID, Date of Birth, and OTP sent to the registered mobile number, then verify details fetched from the OTR database (Name, Date of Birth, Educational Qualification, Community, Gender, Disability, Ex-Servicemen, and Sports status).
Select Examination Centre – Choose the preferred examination centre and preview the application for correctness.
Make Payment – Click SAVE & PAYMENT to pay the application processing fee and examination fee (wherever applicable) via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI.
Download Application – After payment, download the generated PDF application form, which contains a Reference ID Number to be quoted for future correspondence.

Only the details available in the OTR database at the time of application submission will be considered; any changes made to OTR details afterward will not be considered for this notification. For technical issues related to application submission or Hall Ticket downloads, candidates can contact 040-23120301/23120302 or 040-22445566/67445566 (10:30 AM to 5:00 PM on working days) or email [email protected].
important_instructions: Local Status Tie-Breaker: If two or more candidates secure equal total marks, preference in ranking goes first to local candidates, then to date of birth (elder candidate ranked higher), followed by marks in the subject paper and other specified criteria. Medical Fitness: Appointment of selected candidates is subject to being found medically fit, and candidates should be willing to serve anywhere in Telangana State. Anti-Malpractice Provisions: The Telangana Public Examinations (Prevention of Malpractices and Unfair Means) Act, 1997, applies to this examination; violations can lead to debarment, criminal prosecution, and imprisonment of 3 to 7 years along with a fine. Prohibited Items: Mobile phones, smart watches, calculators, Bluetooth devices, pen drives, and other electronic gadgets are strictly banned inside the examination hall. Commission's Decision Final: The Commission's decision on all matters relating to application acceptance, examination conduct, and selection is final and binding on all concerned.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: The complete official notification, including the detailed vacancy break-up (Annexure-I), Scheme and Syllabus (Annexure-II), List of Communities (Annexure-III), Sports quota details (Annexure-IV), and the Normalization criteria (Annexure-VII), has been published by the Commission vide Notification No. 05/G/TP/2026 dated 06 July 2026. Candidates should read the notification carefully before applying, and it can be accessed on the Commission's official website: www.tgpsc.gov.in. Download TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Here

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026 Overview

rows.details: Town and Country Planning Department, Telangana
Objective Type – OMR based Offline or Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT)

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates are advised to submit online applications well before the closing date, since the Commission does not entertain requests to accept applications or extend the deadline after the closing date and time. The exact examination schedule will be notified separately on the Commission's website.

rows.date: 7 days prior to the examination, up to 5:00 PM on the previous day of the examination

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Vacancy Details 2026 (Category-Wise Break-Up)
content_sections.text: A total of 10 vacancies are notified for Assistant Director of Town Planning, distributed across Multi-Zone-I and Multi-Zone-II under Unreserved (UR) and Local (L) sub-categories. Grand Total: 10 posts. Candidates should note that the number of vacancies is provisional and subject to variation on intimation from the concerned department; unfilled or non-joining vacancies will be carried forward to the next recruitment, and there shall be no waiting list.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit for TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Age is reckoned as on 1 July 2026 . The prescribed age limit is 18 to 44 years (the upper age limit having been raised from 34 to 44 years vide G.O.Ms.No.86, G.A.(Ser.A) Department, dated 18/05/2026). Candidates for the minimum age should not be born after 01/07/2008, and for the maximum age should not be born before 02/07/1982. No person is eligible if he/she is less than 18 years of age or has crossed 61 years (superannuation age), even after availing eligible age relaxations.

rows.age_relaxation: Up to 5 years, based on length of regular service
3 years plus length of service rendered in the Armed Forces
3 years plus length of service rendered in the NCC

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification for Assistant Director of Town Planning
content_sections.text: Candidates must possess the prescribed academic qualification as on the date of notification, i.e. 06 July 2026. Minimum qualification of B.Arch., or B.E./B.Tech. (Civil), or B.Planning/B.Tech. (Planning), or M.A. (Geography), or M.Sc. (Geography), or M.Sc. (Geoinformatics); AND A Post Graduate Degree or Diploma in Town and Country Planning from a University in India established or incorporated by or under a Central Act, State Act, or Provincial Act, or Fellowship/Associateship of the Institute of Town Planners of India, or equivalent membership of a professional Institute of Planners recognised by the University Grants Commission. Degrees obtained through Open Universities/Distance Education mode must be recognised by the UGC/AICTE/Distance Education Bureau, as applicable. Candidates claiming an equivalent qualification will have their claim examined by an Expert Committee constituted by the Commission, whose decision is final.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Exam Pattern and Scheme of Examination 2026
content_sections.text: Selection is based on a Written Examination (Objective Type), conducted in OMR-based offline mode or Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT) mode, as decided by the Commission. Paper-I covers Current Affairs, General Science, Environmental Issues and Disaster Management, Economic and Social Development, Geography, History, Indian Constitution and Polity, Social Exclusion and Rights Issues, Telangana Society and Culture, Logical Reasoning and Data Interpretation, and Basic English. Paper-II (Subject Concerned, P.G. Level) covers core Town and Country Planning subjects such as Settlement Evolution and Planning Theory, Planning Analysis and Techniques, Infrastructure Planning, Traffic and Transportation Planning, Housing and Community Planning, Metropolitan and Regional Planning, Urban Information Systems and GIS, Environmental Planning, Urban Governance, Planning Legislation, Urban Design and Conservation, Project Planning and Appraisal, and Disaster Risk Mitigation. Note: Merely securing the minimum qualifying marks does not vest any right in a candidate for being considered for final selection. Scores in multi-session CBRT examinations are normalised as per the formula given in Annexure-VII of the notification, and results are announced on a percentile basis.

rows.paper: Paper-I: General Studies and General Abilities
Paper-II: Subject Concerned (Common to All) (P.G. Level)

content_sections.heading: TGPSC Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: Fee is payable online only, through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. The fee once remitted shall not be refunded or adjusted under any circumstances, and candidature will not be considered unless both the application processing fee and examination fee (wherever applicable) are paid in full.

rows.oc_bc: Rs 120 (all unemployed applicants are exempted; all Government/PSU/Corporation employees must pay this fee)

content_sections.heading: Examination Centres
content_sections.text: The Written Examination (Objective Type) will be held at Hyderabad (including HMDA Jurisdiction), Karimnagar, Khammam, Warangal, and Nizamabad, or such other centres as may be decided by the Commission. Requests for change of examination centre will not be entertained.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy in TGPSC Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Reservation is applicable for Scheduled Tribes, Scheduled Castes, Backward Classes, and Economically Weaker Sections, along with Horizontal Reservation for Persons with Disabilities, Women, and Meritorious Sportspersons, as per Rule 22 and 22(A) of the Telangana State and Subordinate Service Rules, 1996. Applicants belonging to SC, ST, BC, EWS, or differently-abled categories who avail upper age relaxation will also be considered for Open Category (OC) vacancies. No reservation benefit is given on the basis of a temporary Disability certificate. Non-Creamy Layer Certificate (Form-VIIB) is mandatory for BC candidates; in its absence, candidature is considered under Open Competition. EWS reservation requires an Income Certificate for the financial year prior to the year of application, issued by a Revenue Authority not below the rank of Tahsildar. SC/ST candidates must produce a Community Certificate issued by a competent Revenue Authority; applicants belonging to other States are not entitled to any reservation. Meritorious sportsperson reservation (2%) is applicable as per G.O. Ms. No. 74, Youth Advancement, Tourism & Culture (Sports) Department, dated 09/08/2012, and related orders; in the absence of eligible sportspersons, such posts are allotted to open competition.

content_sections.heading: Reservation to Local Candidates
content_sections.text: Since this post is classified as Multi-Zonal, 95% Local Reservation is applicable to all posts notified under Multi-Zone-I and Multi-Zone-II, as per Para-8 of G.O.Ms.No.124, General Administration (SPF-MC) Department, dated 30/08/2018. Local candidate status is determined based on the candidate's period of study or residence in the local area, as defined under the Telangana Public Employment (Organisation of Local Cadres and Regulation of Direct Recruitment) Order, 2018.

rows.districts_covered: Asifabad-Kumrambheem, Mancherial, Peddapalli, Jayashankar Bhupalapalli, Mulugu, Adilabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagityal, Karimnagar, Sircilla-Rajanna, Siddipet, Medak, Kamareddy, Kothagudem-Bhadradri, Khammam, Mahabubabad, Hanumakonda (Warangal Urban), Warangal (Warangal Rural)
Suryapet, Nalgonda, Bhongir-Yadadri, Jangaon, Medchal-Malkajgiri, Hyderabad, Ranga Reddy, Sanga Reddy, Vikarabad, Mahaboobnagar, Narayanpet, Jogulamba-Gadwal, Wanaparthy, Nagar Kurnool

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: One Time Registration (OTR) – First-time applicants must register their bio-data on the TGPSC website to obtain a TGPSC ID; already registered applicants can log in directly using their existing TGPSC ID. Note: OTR is not considered an application for any post. Login & Verify Details – Visit the Online Application link, log in using the TGPSC ID, Date of Birth, and OTP sent to the registered mobile number, then verify details fetched from the OTR database (Name, Date of Birth, Educational Qualification, Community, Gender, Disability, Ex-Servicemen, and Sports status). Select Examination Centre – Choose the preferred examination centre and preview the application for correctness. Make Payment – Click SAVE & PAYMENT to pay the application processing fee and examination fee (wherever applicable) via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Download Application – After payment, download the generated PDF application form, which contains a Reference ID Number to be quoted for future correspondence. Only the details available in the OTR database at the time of application submission will be considered; any changes made to OTR details afterward will not be considered for this notification. For technical issues related to application submission or Hall Ticket downloads, candidates can contact 040-23120301/23120302 or 040-22445566/67445566 (10:30 AM to 5:00 PM on working days) or email [email protected] .

content_sections.heading: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Recent colour passport-size photograph (100–200 KB) and scanned specimen signature (20–30 KB) in JPG/PNG format Proof of educational qualification and SSC/CBSE/ICSE certificate (for Date of Birth) Aadhaar Card/Voter ID/Passport/Driving Licence/PAN Card or other photo ID Community Certificate for SC/ST/BC candidates, and Non-Creamy Layer Certificate (Form-VIIB) for BC candidates Income Certificate for EWS reservation, in the prescribed format Disability Certificate in SADAREM format, for differently-abled applicants No Objection Certificate from employer, for candidates in Government/PSU service Declaration of unemployment, for candidates claiming exemption from examination fee

content_sections.heading: Other Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Local Status Tie-Breaker: If two or more candidates secure equal total marks, preference in ranking goes first to local candidates, then to date of birth (elder candidate ranked higher), followed by marks in the subject paper and other specified criteria. Medical Fitness: Appointment of selected candidates is subject to being found medically fit, and candidates should be willing to serve anywhere in Telangana State. Anti-Malpractice Provisions: The Telangana Public Examinations (Prevention of Malpractices and Unfair Means) Act, 1997, applies to this examination; violations can lead to debarment, criminal prosecution, and imprisonment of 3 to 7 years along with a fine. Prohibited Items: Mobile phones, smart watches, calculators, Bluetooth devices, pen drives, and other electronic gadgets are strictly banned inside the examination hall. Commission's Decision Final: The Commission's decision on all matters relating to application acceptance, examination conduct, and selection is final and binding on all concerned.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: TGPSC Notification No. 05/G/TP/2026 has announced a total of 10 vacancies for the post of Assistant Director of Town Planning in the Town and Country Planning Department.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process starts on 10 July 2026 and closes on 17 August 2026 at 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Assistant Director of Town Planning?
faq.answer: Candidates need a minimum qualification of B.Arch., B.E./B.Tech. (Civil), B.Planning/B.Tech. (Planning), M.A./M.Sc. (Geography), or M.Sc. (Geoinformatics), along with a Post Graduate Degree or Diploma in Town and Country Planning, or equivalent professional membership recognised by the UGC.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 44 years, reckoned as on 1 July 2026, with the upper limit relaxable by 5 years for SC/ST/BC/EWS candidates and up to 10 years for Physically Handicapped persons.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application processing fee is Rs 1,000 for OC and BC candidates and Rs 500 for SC, ST, and PwD candidates, plus an examination fee of Rs 120, which is exempted for unemployed applicants.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning?
faq.answer: Selection is based purely on a Written Examination (Objective Type) comprising Paper-I (General Studies and General Abilities, 150 marks) and Paper-II (Subject Concerned at P.G. level, 300 marks), conducted in OMR or CBRT mode.

faq.question: Q7. What are the qualifying marks required to clear the TGPSC written examination?
faq.answer: The minimum qualifying marks are 40% for OC, Sportspersons, and EWS candidates, 35% for BC candidates, and 30% for SC, ST, and PH candidates.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the TGPSC written examination be conducted?
faq.answer: The examination will be conducted at Hyderabad (including HMDA Jurisdiction), Karimnagar, Khammam, Warangal, and Nizamabad, or at centres decided by the Commission, and requests for centre change are not entertained.

faq.question: Q9. Is Local Reservation applicable to this post?
faq.answer: Yes, since the post is classified as Multi-Zonal, 95% Local Reservation applies to vacancies notified under both Multi-Zone-I and Multi-Zone-II as per G.O.Ms.No.124, General Administration (SPF-MC) Department.

faq.question: Q10. Where can candidates download the TGPSC Assistant Director of Town Planning notification and apply online?
faq.answer: The official notification and the Online Application link are available on the TGPSC website, www.tgpsc.gov.in, where candidates can also complete One Time Registration (OTR) before applying.
